perturbation.original_text: The Applicant agrees that all information disclosed by the Agency to the Applicant by means of a document ‘ ’Technical Specifications for RRMs ’’, including its future amendments, and other documents relating to the RRM registration and data submission shall be considered as confidential (altogether the ‘ Confidential Information’). The Confidential I nformation relates to (without limitation) the Agency’s technical data, actual and anticipated developments or products, know -how, software, hardware, processes, architectures, concepts, ideas, designs, drawings, personnel, financial information, computer programs, studies, work in progress, visual demonstrations, and other data, whether written, graphic, or electronic form.
perturbation.changed_text: The Applicant agrees that all information disclosed by the Agency to the Applicant by means of a document ‘ ’Technical Specifications for RRMs ’’, including its future amendments, and other documents relating to the RRM registration and data submission shall be considered as confidential (altogether the ‘ Confidential Information’). This paragraph applies only if the Applicant has paid all applicable registration fees.
perturbation.explanation: Confidentiality clauses are essential. By placing a condition on it (payment of fees) the confidentiality becomes unclear if fees are disputed, this affects enforceability. The original placement was at the beginning of the contract, thus its importance was clearly stated. By placing such important conditionality, the enforceability of the contract is jeopardized, and thus the change introduces a perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_law: EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) - Article 5(1)(f)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 5(1)(f) of GDPR requires personal data to be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security, including protection against unauthorized or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures. By making confidentiality conditional on fee payment, the agreement fails to ensure data security consistently, creating potential vulnerabilities if payment is disputed.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Art. 5 GDPR – Principles relating to processing of personal data - General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Search for: Skip to content Search for: Imprint | Privacy Policy | Liability Personal data shall be: processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject (‘lawfulness, fairness and transparency’); collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes; further processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes shall, in accordance with Article 89 (1), not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes (‘purpose limitation’); adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed (‘data minimisation’); accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date; every reasonable step must be taken to ensure that personal data that are inaccurate, having regard to the purposes for which they are processed, are erased or rectified without delay (‘accuracy’); kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed; personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89( 1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject (‘storage limitation’); processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures (‘integrity and confidentiality’). The controller shall be responsible for, and be able to demonstrate compliance with, paragraph 1 (‘accountability’). Suitable Recitals ( 39 ) Principles of Data Processing ( 74 ) Responsibility and Liability of the Controller ← Art. 4 GDPR Art. 6 GDPR → GDPR Table of contents Report error
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition (payment of fees) for the confidentiality of information. This contradicts Article 5(1)(f) of GDPR, which requires ensuring appropriate security of personal data, including protection against unauthorized processing. By making confidentiality conditional, the agreement fails to consistently ensure data security, creating vulnerabilities if payment is disputed. The scraped snippet of Art. 5 GDPR states that personal data shall be 'processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures ('integrity and confidentiality').' The modified text weakens this requirement.

perturbation.original_text: The Declaration shall be governed by Union law, complemented, where necessary, by the law of Slovenia. Any dispute between the Agency and the Applicant in relation to the interpretation, application or validity of this Declaration which c annot be settled amicably shall be brought before the courts of Ljubljana.
perturbation.changed_text: This section is for informational purposes only and does not create any binding obligations. Any dispute between the Agency and the Applicant in relation to the interpretation, application or validity of this Declaration which c annot be settled amicably shall be brought before the courts of Ljubljana.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the disclaimer that the section is for informational purposes only, it directly contradicts the provision that disputes will be brought before the courts of Ljubljana. This creates a legal contradiction as it undermines the enforceability of the dispute resolution clause. This change introduces a perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_law: EU Law on Jurisdiction and the Recognition and Enforcement of Judgments in Civil and Commercial Matters (Brussels I Regulation Recast) - Regulation (EU) No 1215/2012
perturbation.law_explanation: The Brussels I Regulation Recast provides a framework for the recognition and enforcement of judgments in civil and commercial matters within the EU. By stating that the dispute resolution clause is 'for informational purposes only,' the agreement undermines the legal certainty required for enforcing judgments under this regulation, potentially invalidating the choice of jurisdiction clause.

perturbation.justification: The original text establishes a clear dispute resolution mechanism, specifying that disputes will be brought before the courts of Ljubljana. The changed text adds a disclaimer stating that the section is for informational purposes only and does not create any binding obligations. This directly contradicts the original provision, undermining the enforceability of the dispute resolution clause. The LLM explanation notes that the Brussels I Regulation Recast requires legal certainty for enforcing judgments. By stating that the dispute resolution clause is 'for informational purposes only,' the changed text introduces uncertainty and thus a contradiction. Scraped Law Snippet 1 confirms the existence of Regulation (EU) No 1215/2012 on jurisdiction and the recognition and enforcement of judgments, which the altered clause undermines. The disclaimer directly negates the binding nature of the dispute resolution clause, creating a significant legal contradiction by potentially invalidating the choice of jurisdiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Applicant 's obligations her eunder shall survive until all information in the Confidential Information hereunder becomes publicly known.
perturbation.changed_text: The Applicant 's obligations her eunder shall survive for a period of 1 year from the date of this declaration.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the duration of the confidentiality obligation to one year, the modification could contradict Union law if the Confidential Information does not become publicly known within that year, potentially leading to unauthorized disclosure. This is a structural flaw as it weakens the indefinite protection that was initially agreed upon, this change introduces a perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_law: EU Directive 2016/943 on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets)
perturbation.law_explanation: Directive 2016/943 aims to harmonize the protection of trade secrets across the EU. By limiting the confidentiality obligation to one year, the modified agreement could fail to adequately protect trade secrets, as the directive intends to safeguard them for as long as they remain confidential and valuable, regardless of a fixed time period.

perturbation.justification: The change from an indefinite confidentiality obligation to a one-year period contradicts the intent of Directive 2016/943, which aims to protect trade secrets for as long as they remain confidential and valuable, as suggested by the Law Explanation. The modified agreement introduces a structural flaw because the original text provided indefinite protection; this indefinite protection is now limited to one year, which is in contradiction with the directive which intends to safeguard them for as long as they remain confidential and valuable, regardless of a fixed time period. Scraped Law Snippet 1 directly references Directive (EU) 2016/943 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 8 June 2016 on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure, thus strengthening the argument that the one-year limitation could lead to unlawful disclosure. The Perturbation Explanation also points out that this undermines the indefinite protection originally agreed upon. Thus, the contradiction is direct and impactful.

perturbation.original_text: The Applicant hereby acknowledges that unauthorised disclosure or use of the Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury, which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, the Applicant agrees that the Agency shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief or an equivalent measure before the competent court , in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have.
perturbation.changed_text: In no event shall either party be liable for any indirect, incidental, consequential, special or exemplary damages arising out of or in connection with this declaration, including, but not limited to, loss of profits or business interruption, even if advised of the possibility of such damages.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly allows for injunctive relief in case of unauthorized disclosure. By introducing a clause that eliminates liability for consequential damages, including loss of profits, it weakens the Agency's ability to seek full compensation for breaches of confidentiality and thus could reduce the impact of the legal actions, this change introduces a perturbation. Liability clauses are critical for enforcing contracts.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Article 45 of the Agreement on Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS Agreement)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 45 of the TRIPS Agreement requires that judicial authorities have the authority to order the infringer to pay the right holder damages adequate to compensate for the injury the right holder has suffered because of an infringement of that person's intellectual property right. By removing the Applicant's liability from potential damages, it limits the ways for the owner of the trade secret to fully repair the damage, thus contradicting the core concept of Article 45 of TRIPS agreement.

perturbation.justification: The original text allows for injunctive relief, while the changed text eliminates liability for consequential damages. The Law Explanation says that Article 45 of the TRIPS Agreement requires judicial authorities to have the authority to order the infringer to pay damages adequate to compensate for the injury. Because the changed text removes the liability for consequential damages, it limits the ways for the owner of the trade secret to fully repair the damage, contradicting the core concept of Article 45 of TRIPS agreement, as noted in the Law Explanation. This constitutes a legal contradiction because it reduces the impact of potential legal actions.
